rulings.Case Title: ANA VEGA VS. OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service Of Summons Due To Lack Of Jurisdiction; Or In The Alternative Request For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action For Inconvient Forum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 10, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANTS OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA, OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS Motion for Order Quashing Service of Summons Due to Lack of Jurisdiction. Motion to quash granted. Plaintiff fails to show the defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California to justify jurisdiction. (See DVI, Inc. vs. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal.App. 4th 1080.) No substantial, continuous, and systematic contacts to justify general jurisdiction. No specific jurisdiction where this complaint arises out of an accident taking place in Nevada, and not from defendants' California-related activities. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA COUNTY SUPERINTENDENTS EDUCATIONAL VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION ON THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney Fees; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Index Of Non Calif. Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 10, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, SHAREN SCOTT Motion For Attorney Fees. Motion for attorneys' fees granted. The claims against the individual defendants were dismissed with prejudice in federal court two months before this state action was filed. Defendants prevailed in this court on the 42 U.S.C. section 1983 claims when they were dismissed with prejudice as res judicata. The claims brought in state court were meritless and without foundation. Attorney fees granted in the lodestar amount of $41,532.50. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA COUNTY SUPERINTENDENTS EDUCATIONAL VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION ON THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended)Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 10, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, and, SHAREN SCOTT (Amended) Motion For Judgment on The Pleadings. Motion denied. Improper motion for reconsideration. (CCP section 1008.) No new facts or law raised from those stated in the demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOAN B HARTLEY VS. GEORGE VALVERDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Of Hearing On Motion For Order Enforcing The Court'S Judgement And/Or Renewed Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 10, 2009, line 12, PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY (Re-Notice) Of Hearing On Motion For Order Enforcing The Court's Judgement and/or Renewed Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Writ petition granted and the matter is remanded for the agency to determine whether the evidence relating to the 2000 alleged prior conviction is sufficient to qualify as a California driving under the influence conviction using the standards set forth in Draeger vs. Reed (1999) 69 Cal.App. 4th 1511, 1521: 1)Wisconsin law is substantially the same as California law on the same offense; 2)the description of the violation is sufficient; and, 3)the interpretation and enforcement of the other state's law are substanitally the same as California law. Exhibit 11 supporting the 2000 prior conviction contains what appears to be a police report that is not an abstract of judgment, and does not ordinarily form part of the record of conviction. (See Droeger at pg. 1523). No clerk's certification appears to incorporate this document that forms the basis for petitioner's "driving," and the remaining documents are ambiguous regarding "driving" or "operating." Sufficient admissible evidence is presented regarding the 2006 prior conviction. Exhibit 12 contains the judgment of conviction, and the criminal complaint that incorporates the statement of probable cause. Request for attorney fees denied, DMV's action not arbitrary or capricious. =(302/CWW)
